zh.St Clement, Jersey: 澤西島聖 克萊門特
zh.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy於1105年出生在法國諾門迪。他於1153年之前與Mrs Robert De Saint Remy在澤西島聖 克萊門特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.St Clement, Jersey: 泽西岛聖 克萊門特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy于1105年出生在法国諾門迪。他于1153年之前与Mrs Robert De Saint Remy在泽西岛聖 克萊門特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Remy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Andre, Jean-Claude, Evens, Germaine, Gisele, Henri, Hugues, Jacques, Jean Raymond, Jean-Daniel, Josee, Laurent.

French (Rémy); West Indian (in Haiti, Martinique, and Guadeloupe Rémy); Walloon and German: from a French personal name which represents a falling together of two distinct Latin names: Remigius (a derivative of remex, genitive remigis ‘rower, oarsman’), and Remedius (from remedium ‘cure, remedy’). The former name was borne by a 6th-century bishop of Rheims; the latter was borne by various other Christian saints of the 8th to 10th centuries. Compare Ramey , Reamy , and Rhamy .

History: The surname Rémy of French origin is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors, once along with its altered forms Ramey and Remey, and once along with the variant de Rémi and altered forms Reamy and Remey. It is also listed in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of Huguenot ancestors was Jacques Rémy, born c. 1630 in France, who by 1672 lived in Westmoreland County, VA, where he died in 1721. Many of his descendants bear the surname Ramey , some also Rhamy . — Léopold Rémy dit Bellefleur from Biécourt in the Vosges married Marie-Angélique Trudel in L'Ange-Gardien, QC, in 1758.

variants.name: Ramey
meanings: Altered form of French Rémy (see Remy ).
French: derivative of Old French ra(i)m ‘branch’ (from Latin ramus), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in a thickly wooded area.

History: This surname (see 1 above) is listed along with its original form Rémy in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors. Many of its bearers are descendants of Jacques Rémy, born c. 1630 in France, who by 1672 lived in Westmoreland County, VA, where he died in 1721.

variants.name: Rhamy
meanings: Altered form of French Rémy (see Remy ).

History: The surname Rhamy is borne by descendants of Richard Remy, born 1781 in Shenandoah County, VA, died 1846 in Ashland County, OH. He was a descendant of Jacques (Jacob) Rémy, a Huguenot from France (see Remy ).

variants.name: Migl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto.

German: perhaps from a pet form of Mieg, which presumably is from a short form of the personal name Remigius (see Remy ). Alternatively, it may be (unrounded) from Müg, a nickname from Middle High German müeje ‘burden, sorrow, hardship’.

variants.name: Remigio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Andres, Angelito, Aurelio, Corazon, Cristino, Eduardo, Emilia, Eulalio, Francisco.
Spanish: from the personal name Remigio, Latin Remigius (see Remy ).

variants.name: Remis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Aleida, Carlos, Domingo, Ignacio, Luis.
Asturian-Leonese (Remís): from a vernacular form of the personal name Remicio, from Latin Remigius (see Remy ).
Slovak (Remiš): from a vernacular short form of the personal name Jeremiáš (see Jeremias ).

variants.name: Reamy
meanings: Probably an altered form of French Rémy (see Remy ) or of one of its altered forms.
English or Irish: unexplained. Compare   Reamey  .
History: This surname is listed along with its original form Rémy in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Remes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Waino.
Finnish: probably from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Remigius (see Remy ). It is found chiefly in eastern Finland.
Slovak (Remeš): from a vernacular short form of the personal name Jeremiáš (see Jeremias ).

variants.name: Remus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Gerd, Hans, Heinz, Helmuth, Horst, Juergen.
German: from a shortened form of the personal name Remigius (see Remy ).

variants.name: Rami
meanings: German (Rämi): from a short form of the medieval Latin personal name Remigius (see Remy ).
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Ramo  , or a habitational name from Rami in Modena province or from any of several other places so called.
Albanian: from Ram (definite form Rami), a short form of the Muslim personal name Ramadan and its Albanian variant Ramazan.

variants.name: Rehm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernd, Otto, Armin, Hans, Gunter, Hermann, Lothar, Oskar.
German: from the medieval personal name Rehm (see Remy ).
German: from Middle High German reme ‘frame, loom, rack’, hence a metonymic occupational name for someone who made or used such items.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy was born in 1105, in Normandy, France. He married Mrs Robert De Saint Remy before 1153, in St Clement, Jersey. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy est né en 1105, à Normandie, France. Il a épousé Mrs Robert De Saint Remy avant 1153, à St Clement, Jersey. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy wurde 1105 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hat vor 1153 in St Clement, Jersey Mrs Robert De Saint Remy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy è nato il nel 1105 nel Normandie, Francia. Lui sposato Mrs Robert De Saint Remy prima del 1153, nel St Clement, Jersey. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Normandie, France: フランスノーマンディ
ja.St Clement, Jersey: ジャージースト クレメント
ja.Wilden, Bedfordshire, England: イギリスベドフォードシュアイアウィルデン
ja.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remyは1105年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた。1153年以前にジャージースト クレメントでMrs Robert De Saint Remyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy(은)는 1105년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그는 1153년 이전 저지 스트 클레멘트에서 Mrs Robert De Saint Remy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy nasceu em 1105, em Normandie, França. Ele casou-se com Mrs Robert De Saint Remy antes 1153, em St Clement, Jérsei. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy родился в 1105 г. в местности норманди, Франция. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Robert De Saint Remy перед 1153 г. в местности ст клемент, Джерси. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Robert I de Saint-Remigius or St Remy nació en 1105, en Normandie, Francia. Se casó con Mrs Robert De Saint Remy antes de 1153, en St Clement, Jersey. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
